revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日比谷神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Hibiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Hibiya jinja(Higashi-Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[:en:Minato City|Minato City]], [[Higashi-Shimbashi]] 2-1-1 | deity={{ill|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} | Festival=Second Friday to Sunday of May | Alternate name=鯖稲荷 (Saba Inari) | Latitude degrees=35|Latitude minutes=39|Latitude seconds=48.0 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=45|Longitude seconds=29.8 | E (East) or W (West)=E | N (North) or S (South)=N | Map=Tokyo city | prefecture={{ill|Minato|qid=Q190088|1=Minato, Tokyo}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.663333|N|139.758278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Hibiya Shrine''' (日比谷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashi-Shinbashi|en|Higashi-Shinbashi|qid=Q5753819|lt=Higashi-Shimbashi|1=Higashi-Shinbashi}}, {{ill|港区|en|港区|qid=Q14219833|lt=Minato City|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, Japan.
The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari deity|1=Inari Ōkami}}) and {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, comprising the four deities {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Sae-no-Kami|ja|瀬織津比売大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaki-tsu-hime|ja|速開都比売大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaki-tsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibuki-do-no-Kami|ja|気吹戸主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki-do-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hayasasurahime|lt=Hayasasurahime|lt=Hayasasura-hime|ja|速佐須良比売大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasura-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Formerly known as Hibiya Inari Myōjin, its parish includes the {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|qid=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome|1=Shiodome}} area.
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unclear. Originally, it was located at Ōtsukayama within what is now {{ill|Hibiya Park|en|Hibiya Park|qid=Q1378533|lt=Hibiya Park|1=Hibiya Park}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} era, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Shibaguchi|ja|芝口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now Higashi-Shimbashi) alongside its parishioners due to the expansion of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|qid=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}. In the Meiji period, it moved to Shimbashi 4-chome due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|qid=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. The entire shrine grounds were later designated for urban redevelopment, prompting another relocation in 2009 to its current site along {{ill|Japan National Route 15|en|Japan National Route 15|qid=Q1045667|lt=National Route 15 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 15}} (Daiichi Keihin) in Higashi-Shimbashi 2-chome.
== Worship == The shrine is also called "Saba Inari" (Mackerel Inari). Initially, at Ōtsukayama, the shrine opened its facilities to aid suffering travelers and prayed for their well-being. The divine efficacy earned it the nickname "Tabinori (Traveler's) Inari Myōjin." After moving to Shimbashi, the name evolved to "Saba Inari," as it became known for curing toothaches. Worshippers would abstain from eating mackerel (saba) while praying at the shrine and offer mackerel as a token of gratitude once their prayers were answered.
The shrine alternates its grand festival with nearby {{ill|Karasumori Shrine|en|Karasumori Shrine|qid=Q11567255|lt=Karasumori Shrine|1=Karasumori Shrine}}, though their parish boundaries do not overlap.
== Parish == * [[:en:Minato City|Minato City]], Shimbashi 2-18 to 2-21, 3-22 to 3-24, 4-5 to 4-21, 4-24 to 4-27, 5-7 to 5-11 * Minato City, Higashi-Shimbashi 1-1, 1-2, 1-5 to 1-10, 2-3, 2-4, 2-8, 2-9, 2-13, 2-14
== Access == The shrine is a four-minute walk from the Karasumori exit of {{ill|Shimbashi Station|en|Shimbashi Station|qid=Q801436|lt=Shimbashi Station|1=Shimbashi Station}} ({{OSM経路図|start=35.66586,139.75800|goal=35.66342,139.75826|name=Route guide}}).
== Notes == A branch shrine of Hibiya Inari Shrine, located at {{ill|Hatchōbori, Chūō, Tokyo|ja|八丁堀 (東京都中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchōbori, Chūō, Tokyo|1=Hatchōbori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390443}} 3-chome (formerly Kyōbashi District, Hibiya Town), is managed as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Tokyo)|ja|日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Tokyo)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4189425}}.
== Related articles == {{Commonscat}}
== External links == * [https://www.hibiyajinja.net/ Hibiya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/05_minato/5003.html Hibiya Shrine] (Tokyo Shrine Association)
{{Shinto Horizontal}}
{{Munakata Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|日比谷神社|version=104065407|comment=Imported full ja history}}
